151.title: Aspekt u kontekstu — parovi koje već znaš
151.intro: B1 starts by reactivating aspect. Every Croatian verb belongs to a pair: the imperfective names the process, the habit, the ongoing action; the perfective names the whole event, its completion, its result. Read each pair inside a real sentence — that is where aspect actually lives.

items.en: to write (be writing) → to write (finish writing)
items.note: Sinoć sam pisao pismo dva sata i napokon sam ga napisao. Prefix na- closes the action.

items.en: to read → to read (through)
items.note: Čitam ovaj roman već tjedan dana; jučer sam pročitao pola. Impf = in progress, pf = got through it.

items.en: to watch → to take a look / watch (to the end)
items.note: Gledali smo film, a poslije smo pogledali i vijesti. po- adds a single, bounded look.

items.en: to study → to learn (master)
items.note: Učim hrvatski svaki dan, ali još nisam naučio sve padeže. na- marks the result: it's learned.

items.en: to cook → to cook (finish)
items.note: Dok sam kuhao ručak, usput sam skuhao i juhu za sutra. s- delivers the finished dish.

items.en: to buy (regularly) → to buy (once)
items.note: Petkom kupujem namirnice; danas sam kupio i cvijeće. Suffix -ovati builds the impf from kupiti.

items.en: to come (keep coming) → to arrive
items.note: Autobus dolazi svakih deset minuta; moj je već došao. A suppletive pair — different stems.

items.en: to speak / talk → to say
items.note: Govorio je dugo, ali nije rekao ništa važno. Another suppletive pair: process vs. single utterance.

items.en: to take (habitually) → to take (once)
items.note: Uzimam lijek svako jutro; jesi li ti uzeo svoj? Suppletive: uzimam / uzeo.

items.en: to open (be opening) → to open (once)
items.note: Trgovina se otvara u osam; danas su je otvorili ranije. Suffix -arati derives the impf.

items.en: to solve (work on) → to solve (crack it)
items.note: Rješavao sam zadatak cijelo jutro i konačno sam ga riješio. -avati impf, -iti pf.

items.en: to be returning → to return (arrive back)
items.note: Vraćam se s posla u pet; jučer sam se vratio kasnije. Reflexive se stays in both aspects.

151.title: Kako biraš vid — proces ili rezultat
151.intro: Aspect isn't decided by the verb list; it's decided by the meaning you want. A handful of signals almost always points to one aspect. Learn the signals and the choice becomes automatic.

items.en: Every morning I read the newspaper.
items.note: Habit / repetition → imperfective. Trigger words: svaki dan, obično, uvijek, često.

items.en: This morning I read the whole newspaper.
items.note: One completed event with a result → perfective. Trigger words: napokon, već, odjednom.

items.hr: Dok sam spavao, zvonio je telefon.
items.en: While I was sleeping, the phone was ringing.
items.note: Two ongoing background actions → imperfective. dok almost always takes the impf.

items.hr: Kad sam ušao, svi su ustali.
items.en: When I came in, everyone stood up.
items.note: One event triggers another → both perfective. kad + pf = a completed moment.

items.en: Tomorrow I'll be writing the report.
items.note: Future as an activity (no promise it's finished) → imperfective infinitive.

items.en: Tomorrow I'll write (finish) the report.
items.note: Future with a guaranteed result → perfective infinitive. The prefix promises completion.

items.en: Don't close the door!
items.note: Negative commands normally take the imperfective: Ne zatvaraj, ne piši, ne otvaraj.

items.en: Close the door!
items.note: A positive one-off command takes the perfective: Zatvori, napiši, otvori.

151.title: Vid u rečenici + slušanje vijesti
151.intro: Choose the aspect the sentence really needs, then test your daily news habit: two questions are read aloud with the text hidden — catch the facts the way you will on the exam.

questions.prompt: Habit, every day: Svako jutro ______ kavu i novine.
questions.explanation: A daily routine takes the imperfective: uzimam. The perfective uzmem would need a dependent clause (Čim uzmem kavu…).

questions.prompt: One completed event: Jučer sam napokon ______ taj roman. (pročitati, male)
questions.explanation: napokon + a finished result → perfective pročitati → pročitao. Impf 'čitao sam' would only describe the process.

questions.prompt: Pick the perfective partner of dolaziti.
questions.explanation: dolaziti / doći is a suppletive pair — the perfective uses a different stem. dolazak is a noun (an arrival).

questions.prompt: Build the sentence: 'While I was cooking, the phone rang.'
questions.answer: Dok sam kuhao, zvonio je telefon
questions.explanation: dok + imperfective for both background actions; the clitic sam sits second in its clause, je second in the main clause.

questions.prompt: A promise it WILL be finished: Sutra ću ______ izvještaj i poslati ti ga.
questions.explanation: A guaranteed result + poslati (pf) → perfective napisati. 'ću pisati' would only mean 'I'll be writing', with no promise of a finished text.

questions.prompt: Negative command (don't do it repeatedly): Ne ______ prozor, hladno je! (zatvarati)
questions.explanation: Negative imperatives take the imperfective: Ne zatvaraj! The perfective Zatvori! is for a single positive command.

questions.prompt: Poslušaj vijest i odaberi točnu tvrdnju.
questions.explanation: The report says the train was delayed because of snow: 'zbog jakog snijega'. Perfective zakasnio marks the completed delay.
questions.audioText: Zbog jakog snijega jutarnji vlak iz Zagreba za Split kasnio je gotovo sat vremena. Putnicima je podijeljen topli čaj dok su čekali na peronu.

questions.prompt: Poslušaj vijest. Koliko je ljudi sudjelovalo?
questions.explanation: The item states 'oko dvije tisuće građana' — around two thousand citizens. Listening for numbers is a core exam skill.
questions.audioText: Na jučerašnjoj humanitarnoj utrci u središtu grada okupilo se oko dvije tisuće građana. Prikupljena sredstva bit će donirana dječjoj bolnici.

152.title: Obitelj glagola: pisati
152.intro: One root, a fistful of prefixes, a whole vocabulary. From pisati Croatian builds at least ten verbs — and each prefix carries a predictable meaning you can reuse on other roots. Learn the family, not the lone verb.

items.en: to write (imperfective base)
items.note: Pišem dnevnik svaku večer. Present: pišem, pišeš, piše… (iotation s → š).

items.en: to write (finish) — na-
items.note: Napisao sam ti dugu poruku. na- = carry the writing to completion. This is the plain perfective of pisati.

items.en: to describe — o-
items.note: Možeš li mi opisati svoj rodni grad? o- = 'write around', i.e. describe. New meaning → new pair (opisati / opisivati).

items.en: to note down, jot down — za-
items.note: Zapiši broj telefona da ga ne zaboraviš. za- = fix in place on paper.

items.en: to sign — pot- (pod-)
items.note: Potpisao sam ugovor jutros. pod- 'under' → write your name underneath. Present: potpišem.

items.en: to copy (out) — pre-
items.note: Učenik je prepisao zadatak s ploče. pre- = transfer across. In medicine also 'prescribe' (but propisati is preferred).

items.en: to write in; to enrol — u-
items.note: Upisao sam se na tečaj hrvatskoga. u- = write into (a register, a course).

items.en: to write out / fill up — iz-
items.note: Ispisao sam cijelu bilježnicu vježbajući padeže. iz- = write out fully, use up.

items.en: to prescribe — pro-
items.note: Liječnica mi je propisala lijek protiv kašlja. pro- 'through, officially' → issue a prescription.

items.en: to describe (imperfective) — secondary pair
items.note: Pisac u romanu opisuje život na selu. From opisati comes the impf opisivati/opisujem.

items.en: to sign (repeatedly) — secondary pair
items.note: Direktor svako jutro potpisuje hrpu dokumenata. Habit → imperfective potpisivati.

items.en: to jot down (as you go) — secondary pair
items.note: Zapisujem nove riječi u bilježnicu dok čitam. Ongoing → zapisivati/zapisujem.

152.title: Prefiksi obitelji pisati
152.intro: Match each prefix to its meaning and drop the right family member into the sentence. If you know the prefix, you can decode the verb.

questions.prompt: 'Please sign here.' — Molim vas, ______ ovdje.
questions.explanation: potpisati (pod- 'under') = to sign. The imperative is potpišite. prepišite = copy, propišite = prescribe.

questions.prompt: 'Note down my number.' — ______ moj broj. (zapisati, imperative)
questions.explanation: zapisati → imperative zapiši. za- fixes the number on paper: jot it down.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to describe'?
questions.explanation: o- 'around' + pisati = opisati, to write around a thing, i.e. describe it.

questions.prompt: 'I enrolled in a Croatian course.' — ______ sam se na tečaj hrvatskoga. (upisati, male)
questions.explanation: upisati se (u- 'into') = to enrol; male participle upisao. Upisao sam se na tečaj.

questions.prompt: The doctor gave you a prescription. Which verb?
questions.explanation: propisati (pro- 'officially') = to prescribe. prepisati would mean to copy it out.

questions.prompt: Build: 'The student copied the exercise from the board.'
questions.answer: Učenik je prepisao zadatak s ploče
questions.explanation: prepisati (pre- 'across') = to copy; s ploče (genitive) = off the board. je stands second.

questions.prompt: Habit, every morning — pick the imperfective: Direktor svako jutro ______ dokumente.
questions.explanation: A repeated action needs the secondary imperfective potpisivati → potpisuje. The perfective potpiše would need a trigger like Čim potpiše…

questions.prompt: 'I have already written you a long message.' — Već sam ti ______ dugu poruku. (napisati, male)
questions.explanation: već + a finished result → perfective napisati → napisao. napisao sam ti poruku.

153.title: Koji glagol iz obitelji pisati? — biranje u kontekstu
153.intro: The exam rarely asks you to list verbs; it asks you to drop the exactly right one into a sentence. Study these minimal contrasts: same root, different prefix or aspect, completely different meaning.

items.hr: Pisao sam pismo. / Napisao sam pismo.
items.en: I was writing a letter. / I wrote (finished) a letter.
items.note: Only aspect differs: process vs. finished result. The second one exists on paper now.

items.en: Describe the city to me. / Write down the address.
items.note: opisati = paint a picture in words; zapisati = record a fact so it isn't lost.

items.en: Sign the contract. / Copy the contract.
items.note: potpisati = put your name to it; prepisati = reproduce its text.

items.hr: Upisao se na fakultet. / Ispisao se s fakulteta.
items.en: He enrolled at university. / He withdrew from university.
items.note: u- 'in' vs. iz- 'out' — opposite directions, opposite meanings.

items.hr: Liječnik je propisao lijek. / Student je prepisao na ispitu.
items.en: The doctor prescribed medicine. / The student copied on the exam.
items.note: propisati (prescribe) vs. prepisati (copy/crib) — one letter, very different situations.

items.hr: Zapisujem riječi dok čitam. / Zapisao sam sve riječi.
items.en: I jot down words as I read. / I wrote down all the words.
items.note: Impf zapisivati for the ongoing habit; pf zapisati for the completed list.

items.hr: Pisac opisuje rat. / Pisac je opisao jednu bitku.
items.en: The writer describes the war. / The writer described one battle.
items.note: opisivati for the extended description; opisati for one bounded scene.

items.hr: Molim vas, napišite mi to. / Molim vas, potpišite mi to.
items.en: Please write that down for me. / Please sign that for me.
items.note: In an office these two get confused constantly — napisati creates text, potpisati authorises it.

items.hr: Cijelu sam bilježnicu ispisao. / Napisao sam samo jednu stranicu.
items.en: I filled the whole notebook. / I wrote just one page.
items.note: ispisati stresses using something up; napisati just completes the writing.

items.en: Add one more sentence (in writing).
items.note: do- = reach / add on. dopisati = write a bit more onto what's there.

153.title: Ubaci pravi glagol
153.intro: Eight sentences, each needing one precise member of the pisati family in the right form. Read the whole sentence before you choose — context decides.

questions.prompt: Na recepciji: 'Molim vas, ______ svoje ime i prezime na obrazac.'
questions.explanation: Writing your name into a form = create text → napisati → napišite. Signing (potpisati) comes at the very bottom.

questions.prompt: Ista situacija, zadnji red: 'A ovdje dolje se ______.'
questions.explanation: The bottom line is for your signature → potpisati → potpišite. Croatian forms distinguish writing your name from signing.

questions.prompt: 'Describe your best friend to me.' — ______ mi svoju najbolju prijateljicu. (opisati, imperative)
questions.explanation: opisati → imperative opiši. o- 'around' → paint her in words.

questions.prompt: 'The doctor prescribed me an antibiotic.' — Liječnica mi je ______ antibiotik.
questions.explanation: propisati (pro-) = to prescribe. prepisati would mean she copied it out; a classic exam trap.

questions.prompt: Ongoing habit: Uvijek ______ nove riječi dok čitam. (zapisivati, 1st sg.)
questions.explanation: Uvijek + a habit → imperfective zapisivati → zapisujem. The perfective zapišem would need a single occasion.

questions.prompt: Build: 'I filled the whole notebook writing exercises.'
questions.explanation: ispisati (iz- 'out, up') = to write out / fill up; clitic sam second. vježbajući = 'while practising' (present verbal adverb).

questions.prompt: Turn imperfective into perfective: 'Pisao sam izvještaj cijeli dan i napokon sam ga ______.'
questions.explanation: The report is finished at last → the plain perfective napisati → napisao. Same root, closing the action.

questions.prompt: Which pair are opposites?
questions.explanation: u- 'in' vs. iz- 'out': to enrol vs. to withdraw. The others are near-synonyms or aspect pairs, not opposites.

154.title: Suvremeni svijet — moderni život i ekrani
154.intro: This is ASOO's biggest B1 theme block, and it powers opinions — the core B1 speaking skill. Learn the vocabulary of modern life, technology and screen habits so you can say what you actually think.

items.en: modern, contemporary
items.note: suvremeno društvo = modern society. Adjective: suvremen, suvremena, suvremeno.

items.en: addiction (to)
items.note: ovisnost o mobitelu, ovisnost o internetu. From ovisiti o = to depend on.

items.en: to be addicted to
items.note: Ovisan sam o kavi. / Ona je ovisna o društvenim mrežama. Takes the locative.

items.en: smartphone / mobile phone
items.note: Everyday word is mobitel; pametni telefon is the fuller term.

items.en: social media (lit. social networks)
items.note: na društvenim mrežama = on social media. Almost always plural.

items.en: standard of living
items.note: Životni standard je porastao. = The standard of living has risen.

items.en: habit
items.note: loša navika = a bad habit; steći naviku = to pick up a habit; G pl. navika.

items.en: screen time
items.note: Literally 'time spent in front of the screen'. provesti vrijeme = to spend time.

items.en: app, application
items.note: skinuti aplikaciju = to download an app; korisna aplikacija = a useful app.

items.en: to post, publish / a post
items.note: objaviti fotografiju = to post a photo; nova objava = a new post.

items.en: follower
items.note: imati tisuću pratitelja = to have a thousand followers. From pratiti = to follow.

items.en: fast-paced lifestyle
items.note: Živimo ubrzanim načinom života. Instrumental for 'in a … way'.

items.en: to take a break from technology
items.note: The 'digital detox' idea: isključiti mobitel = to switch the phone off.

items.en: to focus, concentrate (on)
items.note: Teško se usredotočim uz stalne obavijesti. obavijest = notification.

154.title: Moderni život — riječi i mišljenja
154.intro: Use the theme words in real sentences and forms — the locative after ovisan o, the instrumental of manner, the vocabulary of opinion. One question is spoken with the text hidden.

questions.prompt: 'She is addicted to social media.' — Ona je ovisna ______ društvenim mrežama.
questions.explanation: ovisan/ovisna o + locative: ovisna o društvenim mrežama. Same preposition as ovisiti o.

questions.prompt: 'a bad habit' — loša ______
questions.explanation: navika = habit. loša navika = a bad habit; dobra navika = a good one.

questions.prompt: Which phrase means 'screen time'?
questions.explanation: Literally 'time spent in front of the screen'. radno vrijeme = working hours, slobodno vrijeme = free time.

questions.prompt: Give your opinion: '______ da provodimo previše vremena na mobitelu.' (I think…)
questions.explanation: Mislim da… / Smatram da… = I think / consider that… — the B1 opinion opener.

questions.prompt: 'We live a fast-paced life.' — Živimo ______ načinom života.
questions.explanation: Manner in the instrumental: ubrzanim načinom (masc. instr.). Život too is in the genitive of description.

questions.prompt: Build: 'It's hard for me to concentrate on work.'
questions.answer: Teško mi je usredotočiti se na posao
questions.explanation: Dative experiencer Teško mi je + infinitive; the se stays with usredotočiti se na + accusative (na posao).

questions.prompt: Poslušaj mišljenje i odaberi točan zaključak govornika.
options: Predlaže dan bez mobitela svaki tjedan.
questions.answer: Predlaže dan bez mobitela svaki tjedan.
questions.explanation: The speaker suggests one screen-free day a week ('jedan dan u tjednu bez mobitela'), not banning anything.
questions.audioText: Ne mislim da su mobiteli loši, dapače, jako su korisni. No mislim da bismo svi trebali imati barem jedan dan u tjednu bez mobitela, da se malo odmorimo od ekrana i posvetimo obitelji.

questions.prompt: 'I have a thousand followers.' — Imam tisuću ______. (pratitelj, G pl.)
questions.explanation: After tisuću the noun is genitive plural: pratitelja. From pratiti = to follow.

154.title: Koliko vremena provodimo na mobitelu?
154.intro: Your speaking session for the week: you and your wife compare screen habits. She's a native, so she nudges your phrasing while you defend your opinion. Read your lines ('Me') aloud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jesi li primijetio koliko si sinoć bio na mobitelu? Skoro tri sata!
lines.en: Did you notice how long you were on your phone last night? Almost three hours!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znam, previše. Mislim da polako postajem ovisan o društvenim mrežama.
lines.en: I know, too much. I think I'm slowly getting addicted to social media.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kaže se 'ovisan o društvenim mrežama' — bravo, točan padež! I što misliš zašto?
lines.en: It's 'ovisan o društvenim mrežama' — well done, right case! And why do you think that is?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Kad mi je dosadno, odmah uzmem mobitel. To mi je postala loša navika.
lines.en: When I'm bored, I grab the phone right away. It's become a bad habit for me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Svima nam je. Ali vrijeme provedeno pred ekranom stvarno se zbraja.
lines.en: It's like that for all of us. But screen time really does add up.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se. Zato sam razmišljao da uvedem jedan dan u tjednu bez mobitela.
lines.en: I agree. That's why I was thinking of introducing one screen-free day a week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlična ideja! I umjesto objava tuđih života, malo bismo živjeli svoj.
lines.en: Great idea! And instead of other people's posts, we'd live our own life a bit.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Točno. Teško mi je usredotočiti se kad mi svako malo stigne obavijest.
lines.en: Exactly. It's hard for me to focus when a notification arrives every few minutes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Onda ih isključi! Nije sve što je suvremeno ujedno i korisno.
lines.en: Then switch them off! Not everything modern is also useful.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Imaš pravo. Počnem od nedjelje — mobitel u ladicu, mi u šetnju.
lines.en: You're right. I'll start on Sunday — the phone in the drawer, us out for a walk.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sad govoriš kao pravi Hrvat. I to gramatički gotovo bez greške!
lines.en: Now you're talking like a real Croat. And almost grammatically flawless too!

155.title: Kolumna: 'Živimo li prebrzo?'
155.intro: Columns are an exam reading genre, and guessing unknown words from context is the single highest-value reading strategy. Read this short opinion column line by line; the notes gloss only the hardest words — try to guess before you look.

items.hr: Nekad smo se dopisivali pismima, a danas očekujemo odgovor u sekundi.
items.en: We used to correspond by letters; today we expect a reply in a second.
items.note: dopisivati se = to correspond, write to each other. Guess it from pisati + do-.

items.hr: Tehnologija nam je olakšala život, ali nam je oduzela strpljenje.
items.en: Technology has made life easier for us, but it has taken our patience away.
items.note: olakšati = to make easier (lak = easy); oduzeti = to take away; strpljenje = patience.

items.hr: Mnogi se osjećaju umorno iako cijeli dan ništa 'teško' ne rade.
items.en: Many feel tired even though they do nothing 'hard' all day.
items.note: iako = although. Note the ironic quotation marks around 'teško'.

items.hr: Razlog je jednostavan: mozak je preopterećen informacijama.
items.en: The reason is simple: the brain is overloaded with information.
items.note: preopterećen = overloaded (pre- 'too much' + opteretiti 'to load'). A passive participle.

items.hr: Neprestano provjeravamo poruke i pratimo tuđe živote na mrežama.
items.en: We constantly check messages and follow other people's lives on the networks.
items.note: neprestano = constantly; tuđi = someone else's, other people's.

items.hr: Zbog toga nam je sve teže usredotočiti se na jednu jedinu stvar.
items.en: Because of that it's ever harder for us to focus on a single thing.
items.note: sve teže = harder and harder; jedna jedina = one single. zbog toga = because of that.

items.hr: Stručnjaci savjetuju da svaki dan odvojimo barem sat bez zaslona.
items.en: Experts advise setting aside at least an hour without a screen every day.
items.note: stručnjak = expert; odvojiti = to set aside; barem = at least.

items.hr: U tom se trenutku, tvrde, mozak konačno odmori i napuni baterije.
items.en: In that moment, they claim, the brain finally rests and recharges its batteries.
items.note: tvrditi = to claim, assert; napuniti baterije = to recharge (an idiom here too).

items.hr: Možda rješenje nije u novoj aplikaciji, nego u staroj navici — šetnji.
items.en: Maybe the solution isn't in a new app, but in an old habit — a walk.
items.note: nije… nego = not… but rather; rješenje = solution (from riješiti).

items.hr: Jer ponekad je najbrži način da usporimo upravo — da isključimo mobitel.
items.en: Because sometimes the fastest way to slow down is precisely — to switch off the phone.
items.note: usporiti = to slow down; upravo = precisely; isključiti = to switch off.

155.title: Razumijevanje kolumne + pogađanje iz konteksta
155.intro: Show you understood the column and could infer the hard words. Difficulty climbs from literal comprehension to the author's real stance.

questions.prompt: Prema kolumni, što nam je tehnologija oduzela?
questions.explanation: '…ali nam je oduzela strpljenje.' — technology took away our patience.

questions.prompt: From context, 'dopisivati se' most likely means:
options: to write to each other, correspond
questions.answer: to write to each other, correspond
questions.explanation: 'Nekad smo se dopisivali pismima' — once we corresponded by letters. The reflexive se + pisati signals a mutual action.

questions.prompt: 'preopterećen' contains pre- 'too much' + opteretiti 'to load'. It means:
questions.explanation: mozak je preopterećen informacijama = the brain is overloaded with information. Guessable straight from the parts.

questions.prompt: Complete the experts' advice: Stručnjaci savjetuju da svaki dan ______ barem sat bez zaslona. (odvojiti, 1st pl.)
questions.explanation: da + present for advice/suggestion: da odvojimo = that we set aside. odvojiti sat = to carve out an hour.

questions.prompt: What solution does the column ultimately suggest?
options: Isključiti mobitel i otići u šetnju.
questions.answer: Isključiti mobitel i otići u šetnju.
questions.explanation: '…rješenje nije u novoj aplikaciji, nego u staroj navici — šetnji.' The cure is an old habit: a walk, phone off.

questions.prompt: Rebuild the author's line: 'Technology has made our life easier.'
questions.explanation: Clitics nam je in second position after the subject; olakšati + dative (nam) + accusative (život).

questions.prompt: What is the author's overall stance?
options: Kritičan prema našoj ovisnosti o brzini, ali s praktičnim savjetom.
questions.answer: Kritičan prema našoj ovisnosti o brzini, ali s praktičnim savjetom.
questions.explanation: The tone is critical of our addiction to speed yet constructive — it ends with a doable suggestion, not a rejection of technology.

156.title: Osobno pismo — okvir i korisne fraze
156.intro: The personal letter is an exam writing genre. It has a fixed skeleton — greeting, catch-up, news, plans, sign-off — and a set of reusable phrases. Learn the frame today; you'll fill it with your own life.

items.en: Dear Ana, / Dear Marko,
items.note: Adjective agrees with the name: Draga (f.), Dragi (m.). A comma follows, then a new line.

items.hr: Kako si? Nadam se da si dobro.
items.en: How are you? I hope you're well.
items.note: The standard warm opening. Formal 'vi': Kako ste? Nadam se da ste dobro.

items.en: We haven't been in touch for a long time.
items.note: čuti se = to be in touch. Literally 'we haven't heard each other for long'.

items.hr: Javljam ti se da ti ispričam što ima novoga.
items.en: I'm writing to tell you what's new.
items.note: javiti se = to get in touch; da + present = 'in order to'. što ima novoga = what's new.

items.hr: Kod nas je sve po starom.
items.en: With us everything is as usual.
items.note: po starom = the same as ever. A handy filler between topics.

items.hr: Prošli tjedan dogodilo se nešto zanimljivo.
items.en: Last week something interesting happened.
items.note: Sets up your retold event. dogoditi se = to happen (pf).

items.hr: Napisao sam ti sve u nastavku.
items.en: I've written it all below for you.
items.note: u nastavku = below, in what follows. A pisati-family verb for your letter.

items.en: Soon we're planning to travel to the seaside.
items.note: Your 'plans' paragraph. planirati + infinitive.

items.hr: Nedostaješ mi i jedva čekam da se vidimo.
items.en: I miss you and I can't wait to see you.
items.note: nedostajati + dative (mi); jedva čekam da… = I can't wait to…

items.hr: Moram završiti jer me čeka posao.
items.en: I have to finish because work is waiting for me.
items.note: A natural way to wind down before the sign-off.

items.en: Warm regards,
items.note: The standard friendly closing. More affectionate: Puno pozdrava / Ljubim te.

items.en: Yours, Ivan / Yours, Ana
items.note: Possessive agrees with the writer's gender: Tvoj (m.), Tvoja (f.).

items.en: Write to me soon!
items.note: pisati → imperative piši. A friendly P.S. line.

items.en: Lots of greetings and hugs.
items.note: pozdravljati + accusative (te). grliti = to hug — very warm register.

156.title: Sastavljanje pisma
156.intro: Assemble the letter's pieces correctly: agreement in the greeting and sign-off, the right pisati-family verb, an aspect pair in your news. Build it as you would write it.

questions.prompt: Writing to your friend Ana, the greeting is:
questions.explanation: The adjective agrees with a feminine name: Draga Ana. For Marko it would be Dragi Marko.

questions.prompt: Warm opener: Kako si? ______ se da si dobro. (I hope)
questions.explanation: nadati se = to hope: Nadam se da si dobro. Reflexive se in second position.

questions.prompt: 'I've written it all below for you.' — ______ sam ti sve u nastavku.
questions.explanation: napisati = to write (and finish). potpisao = signed, prepisao = copied, upisao = enrolled — wrong meanings here.

questions.prompt: Aspect in your news — a completed event: Prošli tjedan ______ se nešto zanimljivo.
questions.explanation: A single completed happening → perfective dogoditi se → dogodilo se. The impf događalo se would mean 'kept happening'.

questions.prompt: 'I miss you.' — ______ mi. (nedostajati, 2nd sg. subject 'you')
questions.explanation: nedostajati takes a dative experiencer (mi) and agrees with the nominative subject 'you': Nedostaješ mi.

questions.prompt: Build the closing line: 'I can't wait to see you.'
questions.explanation: jedva čekam da + present: Jedva čekam da se vidimo = I can't wait for us to see each other.

questions.prompt: Ana signs off her own letter. Which is correct?
questions.explanation: Srdačan pozdrav is fixed (masc. sg.); the possessive matches the writer Ana → tvoja Ana.

questions.prompt: Friendly P.S.: ______ mi uskoro! (pisati, imperative)
questions.explanation: pisati → imperative piši (iotation s → š). Piši mi uskoro! = Write to me soon!

156.title: Žena čita moje pismo
156.intro: You've drafted the letter; now your wife proofreads it aloud. She catches two typical mistakes — an aspect slip and a case ending — and praises the rest. Read your lines ('Me').

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Napisao sam pismo svom prijatelju. Možeš li ga pregledati?
lines.en: I wrote a letter to my friend. Could you look it over?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Naravno, daj da vidim. 'Dragi Marko, kako si?' — savršen početak.
lines.en: Of course, let me see. 'Dear Marko, how are you?' — a perfect opening.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hvala. Bojao sam se da sam pogriješio padež negdje.
lines.en: Thanks. I was afraid I'd got a case wrong somewhere.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Skoro sve je točno. Ovdje: 'putovali smo na more' — a to još niste, planirate.
lines.en: Almost everything's right. Here: 'we travelled to the sea' — but you haven't yet, you're planning to.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aha, znači trebam nesvršeni vid u budućnosti: 'planiramo putovati'.
lines.en: Ah, so I need the imperfective in the future: 'we're planning to travel'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Točno tako. I još sitnica: 'nedostaješ mi', ne 'nedostaje mi ti'.
lines.en: Exactly. And one more little thing: 'nedostaješ mi', not 'nedostaje mi ti'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Da, glagol se slaže s onim tko nedostaje, a ja sam u dativu. Zaboravim to.
lines.en: Right, the verb agrees with the one who's missed, and I'm in the dative. I forget that.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sad znaš. Kraj ti je predivan: 'Jedva čekam da se vidimo, tvoj Ivan.'
lines.en: Now you know. Your ending is lovely: 'I can't wait to see each other, yours, Ivan.'

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Onda samo ispravim te dvije greške i pismo je gotovo.
lines.en: Then I'll just fix those two mistakes and the letter's done.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Iskreno, piše bolje od mnogih Hrvata. Ponosna sam na tebe!
lines.en: Honestly, it reads better than many Croats' writing. I'm proud of you!

157.title: Tjedni pregled — obitelj pisati, vid i moderni život
157.intro: Week 22 in one page. Reread these consolidated examples aloud: the pisati family, aspect chosen by meaning, and the modern-life vocabulary — everything you'll need in today's long conversation.

items.hr: Napisao sam pismo i potpisao ga.
items.en: I wrote the letter and signed it.
items.note: Two pisati-family perfectives in a row: napisati (finish) + potpisati (sign).

items.hr: Opiši mi grad, a ja ću zapisati imena ulica.
items.en: Describe the city to me, and I'll note down the street names.
items.note: opisati vs zapisati — describe vs. record; futur I: ja ću zapisati.

items.hr: Svaki dan čitam vijesti, ali danas sam pročitao cijeli članak.
items.en: Every day I read the news, but today I read a whole article.
items.note: Habit (impf čitam) vs. one completed act (pf pročitao).

items.hr: Dok sam se vraćao kući, nazvao me prijatelj.
items.en: While I was returning home, a friend called me.
items.note: dok + impf (vraćao se) as background; pf nazvao as the single event.

items.en: I'm becoming a little addicted to my phone.
items.note: ovisan o + locative; postajati = to be becoming.

items.hr: Provodimo previše vremena na društvenim mrežama.
items.en: We spend too much time on social media.
items.note: provoditi vrijeme = to spend time; na + locative (mrežama).

items.hr: Životni standard je porastao, ali nam je nestalo strpljenja.
items.en: The standard of living has risen, but we've run out of patience.
items.note: porasti (pf) = to rise; nestati + genitive (strpljenja) = to run out.

items.hr: Mislim da bismo trebali odvojiti sat bez zaslona.
items.en: I think we should set aside an hour without a screen.
items.note: Mislim da + kondicional bismo trebali — a polished opinion frame.

items.hr: Kad se usredotočim, brzo sve završim.
items.en: When I focus, I finish everything quickly.
items.note: usredotočiti se (pf) in a kad-clause; a neat perfective-present.

items.hr: Suvremeni život ima svoje dobre i loše strane.
items.en: Modern life has its good and bad sides.
items.note: A ready-made balanced sentence for opinions: dobre i loše strane.

157.title: Miješani tjedni test
157.intro: One pass across the whole week — pisati family, aspect, modern-life words, letter phrases. One item is spoken with the text hidden, like a mini listening task.

questions.prompt: 'Note down the address so we don't forget it.' — ______ adresu da je ne zaboravimo.
questions.explanation: za- = fix on paper → zapisati. Opiši = describe, Potpiši = sign, Prepiši = copy.

questions.prompt: Habit: Svaki dan ______ vijesti na mobitelu. (čitati, 1st sg.)
questions.explanation: Svaki dan → imperfective čitam. The perfective pročitam would need a single occasion.

questions.prompt: 'I'm becoming addicted to my phone.' — Postajem ovisan ______ mobitelu.
questions.explanation: ovisan o + locative: ovisan o mobitelu. Same o as in ovisiti o.

questions.prompt: Aspect: 'Yesterday I finally read the whole article.' — Jučer sam napokon ______ cijeli članak.
questions.explanation: napokon + a finished whole → perfective pročitati → pročitao.

questions.prompt: Build: 'While I was returning home, a friend called me.'
questions.answer: Dok sam se vraćao kući, nazvao me prijatelj
questions.explanation: dok + impf (vraćao se) for the background; clitics sam se second; pf nazvao for the single event, me second in its clause.

questions.prompt: Poslušaj rečenicu i odaberi točan prijevod.
options: We spend little time at work.
We spend too much time on social media.
We often travel to the seaside.
questions.answer: We spend too much time on social media.
questions.explanation: The sentence is 'Provodimo previše vremena na društvenim mrežama' — spending too much time on social media.
questions.audioText: Provodimo previše vremena na društvenim mrežama, a premalo u stvarnom razgovoru s ljudima oko nas.

questions.prompt: Polished opinion: Mislim da ______ trebali odvojiti sat bez zaslona. (kondicional, 1st pl.)
questions.explanation: 1st person plural conditional is bismo: Mislim da bismo trebali… Most natives get this form wrong — you won't.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is fully correct?
options: Suvremen život ima svoje dobre i loše strane.
Suvremeni život ima svoji dobri i loši strani.
Suvremeni život ima svoje dobre i loše strana.
Suvremeni život ima svoje dobre i loše strane.
questions.answer: Suvremeni život ima svoje dobre i loše strane.
questions.explanation: Definite adjective suvremeni + accusative plural object svoje dobre i loše strane. The others break agreement or the case.

157.title: Dugi razgovor: moderni život i navike
157.intro: The week's long conversation. You and your wife range over modern life, habits and how much has changed — she keeps it flowing and slips in gentle praise. Read your lines ('Me') at a natural pace.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Reci mi iskreno — misliš li da danas živimo bolje nego naši roditelji?
lines.en: Tell me honestly — do you think we live better today than our parents did?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: S jedne strane da, životni standard je porastao. S druge strane, nešto smo izgubili.
lines.en: On one hand yes, the standard of living has risen. On the other, we've lost something.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zanimljivo. A što to točno misliš da smo izgubili?
lines.en: Interesting. And what exactly do you think we've lost?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Strpljenje i mir. Prije su se ljudi dopisivali pismima, a mi očekujemo odgovor odmah.
lines.en: Patience and calm. People used to write to each other by letter, and we expect a reply instantly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Istina. Meni je ponekad teško i pročitati knjigu do kraja, a nekad sam gutala romane.
lines.en: True. Sometimes it's even hard for me to finish a book, and I used to devour novels.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: I meni. Čim sjednem čitati, uzmem mobitel i izgubim koncentraciju.
lines.en: Me too. The moment I sit down to read, I grab the phone and lose concentration.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zato smo se dogovorili za dan bez ekrana. Jesi li se držao dogovora?
lines.en: That's why we agreed on a screen-free day. Did you stick to the deal?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jesam, u nedjelju. Iskreno, prvi sat mi je bilo teško, a poslije mi je bilo divno.
lines.en: I did, on Sunday. Honestly, the first hour was hard for me, and afterwards it felt wonderful.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vidiš! Ne mora sve što je suvremeno biti i bolje za nas.
lines.en: You see! Not everything modern has to be better for us.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Slažem se. Mislim da bismo trebali češće isključiti mobitele i više razgovarati.
lines.en: I agree. I think we should switch our phones off more often and talk more.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kao sada. I primijetila sam — cijeli razgovor bez ijedne veće greške!
lines.en: Like now. And I've noticed — the whole conversation without a single major mistake!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: To je zato što imam najbolju učiteljicu kod kuće. Hvala ti na strpljenju.
lines.en: That's because I have the best teacher at home. Thank you for your patience.

158.title: Obitelj glagola: gledati
158.intro: The gledati family shows how much a single prefix does: from 'watch' you get 'examine', 'sightsee', 'catch sight of' and even 'appear'. The prefix carries the meaning — decode it once and you own the whole set.

items.en: to watch, look at (imperfective base)
items.note: Gledam film. / Gledaj me kad govorim. Present: gledam, gledaš…

items.en: to take a look, glance — po-
items.note: Pogledaj ovo! po- = a single, bounded look. Also the plain perfective of gledati (watch to the end).

items.en: to examine, review, check — pre-
items.note: Liječnik me pregledao. pre- = go over thoroughly. Also: pregledati e-poštu = check your email.

items.en: to look around, sightsee — raz-
items.note: Razgledali smo stari grad. raz- = look all around, in every direction.

items.en: to catch sight of, spot — u-
items.note: Ugledao sam more s vrha brda. u- = a sudden, single perception: it comes into view.

items.en: to look, appear — iz-
items.note: Dobro izgledaš danas! Imperfective only; describes appearance, not an action. Izgleda da… = It seems that…

items.en: to supervise, oversee — nad-
items.note: Šef nadgleda cijeli projekt. nad- 'over' → keep watch over.

items.en: to look at oneself, look around — o-
items.note: Ogledala se u izlogu. o- + se → look at/around oneself; also 'be reflected'.

items.en: to examine / check (imperfective) — secondary pair
items.note: Svako jutro pregledavam poštu. From pregledati comes the impf pregledavati/pregledavam for the habit.

items.en: to go sightseeing (imperfective) — secondary pair
items.note: Cijeli smo dan razgledavali muzeje. Ongoing sightseeing → razgledavati.

items.en: It looks like it's going to rain.
items.note: Izgleda da… = It appears that… — a very common impersonal opener.

items.en: 'Glance at' and 'examine' — not the same.
items.note: pogledati = a quick look; pregledati = a careful, thorough inspection. Don't mix them.

158.title: Prefiksi obitelji gledati
158.intro: Pick the family member each situation calls for — a glance, a thorough check, a sightseeing tour, a sudden spotting, or plain appearance.

questions.prompt: 'The doctor examined me thoroughly.' — Liječnik me temeljito ______.
questions.explanation: pre- = go over thoroughly → pregledati, a medical examination. pogledao would be just a glance.

questions.prompt: 'You look great today!' — Odlično ______ danas! (izgledati, 2nd sg.)
questions.explanation: izgledati = to appear/look; 2nd sg. izgledaš. It describes appearance, never an action.

questions.prompt: Tourists in Dubrovnik: 'We spent the whole day ______ the old town.'
questions.explanation: raz- = look all around → razgledati/razgledavati = to sightsee. The verbal adverb razgledavajući = 'while sightseeing'.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to check your email'?
questions.explanation: pregledati (pre-) = to go through/check → pregledati e-poštu. The others don't collocate with email.

questions.prompt: Sudden spotting: S vrha brda ______ sam more. (ugledati, male)
questions.explanation: u- = come into view → ugledati = to catch sight of; male participle ugledao. The sea suddenly appears.

questions.prompt: Build: 'It looks like it's going to rain.'
questions.explanation: Izgleda da… = It seems that…; futur I će padati; subject kiša at the end. A fixed impersonal frame.

questions.prompt: Habit vs. one-off — pick the imperfective: Svako jutro ______ poštu prije posla.
questions.explanation: Svako jutro → the secondary imperfective pregledavati → pregledavam. The perfective pregledam would need a single occasion.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the RIGHT verb?
questions.explanation: nad- 'over' → nadgledati = to supervise/oversee. ugleda = catches sight of, izgleda = appears, razgleda = sightsees — all wrong here.

159.title: Nezgodni prezenti I: ostanem, uzmem, počnem
159.intro: A small set of high-frequency verbs hides an irregular present stem. -ati verbs like ostati/postati go to -anem; uzeti and početi are suppletive. Drill them once, properly — they turn up constantly in dependent clauses and futures.

items.en: to stay → I stay
items.note: Full present: ostanem, ostaneš, ostane, ostanemo, ostanete, ostanu. -ati infinitive, -anem present.

items.hr: Večeras ostanem doma i gledam film.
items.en: Tonight I'll stay home and watch a film.
items.note: Perfective ostati in a habitual/planned present — very common in speech.

items.en: to become → I become
items.note: postanem, postaneš, postane, postanemo, postanete, postanu. + nominative: Postat ću liječnik → Kad postanem liječnik…

items.en: to stop / stand → I stop
items.note: stanem, staneš, stane… Autobus stane pred kućom. Don't confuse with the family below.

items.en: to stop (doing) → I stop
items.note: prestanem, prestaneš, prestane… Ako prestanem pušiti, bit ću zdraviji. prestati + infinitive.

items.en: to get up → I get up
items.note: ustanem, ustaneš, ustane… Ustanem u šest i odmah popijem vodu.

items.en: to take → I take
items.note: Suppletive! uzmem, uzmeš, uzme, uzmemo, uzmete, uzmu. Uzmem kavu i novine svako jutro.

items.en: to begin → I begin
items.note: Suppletive! počnem, počneš, počne, počnemo, počnete, počnu. početi + infinitive: Počnem raditi u osam.

items.hr: Čim uzmem ključeve, ustanem i krenem.
items.en: As soon as I take the keys, I get up and set off.
items.note: Three of today's presents chained after čim ('as soon as') — the natural home of perfective presents.

items.en: When I start, I can't stop.
items.note: počnem (početi) and stati/stanem together — note both irregular presents.

items.hr: Ako ostanemo, propustit ćemo zadnji autobus.
items.en: If we stay, we'll miss the last bus.
items.note: 1st pl. ostanemo. Perfective present after ako in a real condition.

159.title: Konjugacija nezgodnih prezenta
159.intro: Conjugate the tricky stems and place them in dependent clauses, where perfective presents actually live. The ramp goes from single forms to full sentences.

questions.prompt: ja-oblik: Ako je hladno, ______ doma. (ostati)
questions.explanation: ostati → ostanem (-ati infinitive, -anem present). Perfective present after ako.

questions.prompt: 'I take a coffee every morning.' — Svako jutro ______ kavu.
questions.explanation: uzeti has the suppletive present uzmem, uzmeš, uzme… (the imperfective uzimam also works for a pure habit).

questions.prompt: 'I start work at eight.' — ______ raditi u osam. (početi, 1st sg.)
questions.explanation: početi → suppletive present počnem, počneš, počne… početi + infinitive raditi.

questions.prompt: 3rd sg.: Autobus ______ točno pred kućom.
questions.explanation: stati → 3rd sg. stane. (The impf staje 'keeps stopping' is possible but the perfective stane fits a single scheduled stop.)

questions.prompt: 1st plural: Ako ______, propustit ćemo autobus. (ostati)
questions.explanation: ostati → 1st pl. ostanemo. Real condition: ako + perfective present, main clause futur I.

questions.prompt: 'If I stop smoking, I'll be healthier.' — Ako ______ pušiti, bit ću zdraviji.
questions.explanation: prestati → prestanem. A real condition needs the perfective present after ako; prestati + infinitive pušiti.

questions.prompt: Build: 'As soon as I take the keys, I set off.'
questions.explanation: čim ('as soon as') + perfective present uzmem; the main clause has another perfective present krenem.

questions.prompt: Which full conjugation is correct for postati?
options: postanem, postaneš, postane, postanemo, postanete, postanu
postajem, postaješ, postaje, postajemo, postajete, postaju
postam, postaš, posta, postamo, postate, postaju
postnem, postneš, postne, postnemo, postnete, postnu
questions.answer: postanem, postaneš, postane, postanemo, postanete, postanu
questions.explanation: Perfective postati → postanem…postanu (-anem type). The set postajem…postaju is the imperfective postajati.

160.title: Znanost i izumi — riječi za čitanje biografije
160.intro: The exam's Reading A is a ~150-word biography with open questions. Learn the science vocabulary that runs through every such text, so the content — not the words — is what you focus on.

items.en: science
items.note: znanstveni = scientific; prirodne znanosti = natural sciences. Feminine i-declension: G znanosti.

items.en: scientist
items.note: The person who does znanost. Also istraživač = researcher.

items.en: invention
items.note: The thing invented. G pl. izuma. Related verb: izumiti.

items.en: inventor
items.note: The person who invents. Penkala je bio izumitelj = Penkala was an inventor.

items.en: to invent (pf)
items.note: Izumio je mehaničku olovku. Impf: izumljivati. iz- 'out' → bring out a new thing.

items.en: discovery
items.note: znanstveno otkriće = a scientific discovery. Neuter: G otkrića. Verb: otkriti.

items.en: to discover (pf)
items.note: Otkrio je novi element. Impf: otkrivati. Distinct from izumiti: you discover what exists, invent what didn't.

items.en: research
items.note: A verbal noun from istraživati. baviti se istraživanjem = to do research.

items.en: experiment
items.note: provesti pokus = to carry out an experiment. Synonym: eksperiment.

items.en: patent / to patent
items.note: Patentirao je svoj izum = He patented his invention. patentirati is bi-aspectual.

items.en: device, apparatus
items.note: A concrete piece of equipment. G pl. uređaja.

items.en: award, prize
items.note: dobiti nagradu = to win an award; Nobelova nagrada = the Nobel Prize.

160.title: Biografija: Slavoljub Penkala
160.intro: A biography in the exact exam genre — short sentences, dates, achievements, one passive participle. Read it closely; the exercise will ask who, when and what.

items.hr: Slavoljub Eduard Penkala rodio se 1871. godine.
items.en: Slavoljub Eduard Penkala was born in 1871.
items.note: roditi se = to be born. Dates: 1871. godine (ordinal + godine, genitive).

items.hr: Već je kao mladić pokazivao veliko zanimanje za tehniku.
items.en: Even as a young man he showed a great interest in technology.
items.note: pokazivati zanimanje za = to show interest in (+ accusative).

items.hr: Preselio se u Zagreb, gdje je proveo veći dio života.
items.en: He moved to Zagreb, where he spent most of his life.
items.note: preseliti se = to move (house); provesti = to spend (time).

items.en: In 1906 he invented the mechanical pencil.
items.note: Godine 1906. = In 1906; izumiti = to invent. The famous 'penkala'.

items.hr: Ubrzo je patentirao i prvo nalivpero na svijetu.
items.en: Soon he also patented the world's first fountain pen.
items.note: ubrzo = soon; nalivpero = fountain pen; na svijetu = in the world.

items.hr: Njegovi su izumi bili prodavani u više od sedamdeset zemalja.
items.en: His inventions were sold in more than seventy countries.
items.note: bili prodavani = were (being) sold — a passive with the participle prodavan. više od + genitive.

items.hr: Osim toga, konstruirao je i prvi hrvatski avion.
items.en: In addition, he also built the first Croatian aeroplane.
items.note: osim toga = besides that; konstruirati = to construct, design.

items.hr: Osnovao je tvornicu koja je zapošljavala mnogo radnika.
items.en: He founded a factory that employed many workers.
items.note: osnovati = to found; zapošljavati = to employ (impf, ongoing).

items.en: He died in 1922 in Zagreb.
items.note: umrijeti → umro (m. participle). Locative u Zagrebu.

items.hr: Danas mnoge škole i ulice nose njegovo ime.
items.en: Today many schools and streets bear his name.
items.note: nositi ime = to bear a name; njegovo ime = his name (neuter).

160.title: Razumijevanje biografije + znanstvene vijesti
160.intro: Answer who/when/what in full forms, exactly as the exam wants, and handle one spoken science headline with the text hidden.

questions.prompt: Kada se Penkala rodio?
questions.explanation: 'Penkala se rodio 1871. godine.' The 1906 and 1922 dates are his invention and his death.

questions.prompt: Što je Penkala izumio 1906. godine?
questions.explanation: In 1906 he invented the mechanical pencil ('penkala'). The fountain pen came soon after (ubrzo).

questions.prompt: U kojem je gradu Penkala proveo veći dio života? Proveo ga je u ______. (locative)
questions.explanation: u + locative: u Zagrebu. He moved there and spent most of his life in the city.

questions.prompt: What does the passive 'bili prodavani' tell us?
options: He was selling the inventions himself.
The inventions were sold (by others, in many countries).
questions.answer: The inventions were sold (by others, in many countries).
questions.explanation: biti + passive participle prodavani = a passive: the inventions were sold, without naming the seller.

questions.prompt: Vocabulary: a person who invents things is an ______. (Croatian, nominative sg. masc.)
questions.explanation: izumitelj = inventor (from izumiti). A scientist more broadly is a znanstvenik.

questions.prompt: otkriti vs. izumiti — which is right? 'Kolumbo je ______ Ameriku.'
questions.explanation: You discover (otkriti) what already exists; you invent (izumiti) what didn't. Columbus discovered → otkrio.

questions.prompt: Poslušaj znanstvenu vijest i odaberi glavnu činjenicu.
options: Hrvatski su znanstvenici razvili novi medicinski uređaj.
questions.answer: Hrvatski su znanstvenici razvili novi medicinski uređaj.
questions.explanation: The item reports that Croatian scientists have developed a new medical device that will help patients.
questions.audioText: Skupina hrvatskih znanstvenika s Instituta Ruđer Bošković razvila je novi medicinski uređaj koji bi mogao ubrzati postavljanje dijagnoze i tako pomoći tisućama pacijenata.

questions.prompt: Build: 'Today many streets bear his name.'
questions.answer: Danas mnoge ulice nose njegovo ime
questions.explanation: nositi ime = to bear a name; subject mnoge ulice (nom. pl.), object njegovo ime (acc. sg. neuter).
